title: Rename PolicyPoint four-class labels to Yes and No
description:
Update the PolicyPoint Sales Skills QA score versions that currently use Met / Partially Met / Missed / NA.

Required behavior:
- Rename Met to Yes.
- Rename Missed/not met to No.
- Retain Partially Met and NA.
- Regenerate matching guidelines and score configs.
- Validate guidelines with `skills/guidelines/validate_guidelines.py`.
- Publish and promote updated score versions for the affected scores only.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Started label correction. Existing setup payload is available. Affected scores are the 8 four-class classifiers; the 2 existing Yes / No / NA qualifying question scores are already aligned and will not be changed.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Validated 8 regenerated guideline files and 8 regenerated score configs. Created 8 new score versions with the corrected classes: Yes / No / Partially Met / NA. Next step is champion promotion and read-back verification.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Completed label correction. Published and promoted updated champion versions for all 8 four-class PolicyPoint Sales Skills scores. Final verification confirmed champion configs and code classes are exactly `Yes`, `No`, `Partially Met`, `NA`; guidelines match the generated payload; all promoted versions are featured; old `Met` / `Missed` headings are absent. The 2 binary Yes / No / NA scores were unchanged.
